data.title: Fastly Stock Soars 40% After Q4 Earnings Beat, Strong Outlook
data.description:
Fastly, Inc. (NASDAQ:FSLY) shares are trading higher Thursday after the company reported better-than-expected fourth-quarter financial results and issued fiscal-year 2026 and first-quarter guidance above estimates on Wednesday after the market closed.

  • Fastly stock is approaching key resistance levels. Why did FSLY hit a new high?

Fourth-Quarter Performance And Outlook

Fastly posted adjusted earnings per share of 12 cents, beating the consensus estimate of 6 cents. In addition, it reported revenue of $172.61 million, beating the consensus estimate of $161.36 million.

Total revenue increased 23% year-over-year. Network services revenue rose 19% to $130.8 million, while security revenue climbed 32% to $35.4 million. Other revenue grew 78% to $6.4 million.

Remaining performance obligations were $354 million, up 55% year over year. Enterprise customer count increased to 628, up 32 from the fourth quarter of 2024.

Fastly's last 12-month net retention rate increased to 110% in the fourth quarter from 106% in the third quarter of 2025.

Shares Climb Above Key Averages Amid Mixed Momentum

Fastly is currently trading 44.4% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 37.2% above its 100-day SMA, demonstrating strong short-term momentum. Over the past 12 months, shares have decreased by 7.55%, and they are currently positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows, suggesting a potential recovery phase.

The RSI is at 48.96, indicating neutral momentum, while the MACD is above its signal line, suggesting bullish conditions. The combination of neutral RSI and bullish MACD suggests mixed momentum, indicating potential for upward movement if market conditions remain favorable.

  • Key Resistance: $14.00
  • Key Support: $12.50

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Fastly, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Momentum: Neutral (Score: 56.53) — The stock is showing moderate performance indicators.

The Verdict: Fastly’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a mixed performance outlook. While the Momentum score indicates some strength, the overall market positioning suggests traders should remain cautious as they monitor further developments.

Fastly Shares Skyrocket Higher

FSLY Price Action: At the time of writing, Fastly shares are trading 39.96% higher at $12.98, according to data from Benzinga Pro.

data.title: Here's How Much $1000 Invested In Marriott International 15 Years Ago Would Be Worth Today
data.description:

Buying $1000 In MAR: If an investor had bought $1000 of MAR stock 15 years ago, it would be worth $8,758.54 today based on a price of $358.34 for MAR at the time of writing.

data.title: Applovin Stock Falls Despite Q4 Earnings And Revenue Beat
data.description:
Applovin Corporation (NASDAQ:APP) shares are trading lower Thursday. The company reported fourth-quarter financial results on Wednesday after the market closed.

  • AppLovin stock is feeling bearish pressure. Why are APP shares down?

Fourth-Quarter Results And Outlook

Applovin reported earnings per share of $3.24, beating the consensus estimate of $2.95. In addition, the company reported revenue of $1.65 billion, beating the consensus estimate of $1.60 billion.

Total revenue increased 66% year-over-year. Cash flow from operations and free cash flow both totaled $1.31 billion in the quarter.

During the quarter, Applovin repurchased $481.7 million of its common stock and ended the period with approximately $2.49 billion in cash and cash equivalents.

Applovin sees first-quarter revenue of $1.74 billion to $1.77 billion, versus the consensus estimate of $1.69 billion.

Analyst Consensus And Recent Actions

Applovin has a consensus rating of Buy and a consensus price target of $701.71. Recent actions include:

  • BTIG analyst Clark Lampen maintained a Buy rating on AppLovin and lowered the price target from $771 to $640.
  • Needham analyst Bernie McTernan reiterated a Buy rating on AppLovin and maintained a $700 price target.

Technical Indicators Point To Short-Term Weakness, Mixed Momentum

The stock is currently trading 16.3% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 30.5% below its 50-day SMA, indicating a bearish trend in the short to medium term. Over the past 12 months, shares have increased by 20.11%, suggesting a stronger long-term performance despite recent challenges. Currently, the stock is positioned closer to its 52-week lows than highs, indicating potential weakness.

The RSI is at 40.57, which is considered neutral territory, while the MACD is below its signal line, indicating bearish pressure on the stock. The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum, reflecting uncertainty among traders.

  • Key Resistance: $429.00
  • Key Support: $385.00

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Applovin, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value Rank: 7.26 — The stock is trading at a steep premium relative to peers.
  • Growth Rank: 37.83 — Indicates moderate growth potential.
  • Momentum Rank: 56.53 — The stock is showing neutral momentum.

The Verdict: Applovin’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a mixed outlook. While the Growth (37.83) and Momentum (56.53) scores indicate some potential, the Value (7.26) score warns that the stock is trading at a premium, suggesting caution for investors.

Applovin Shares Slide Lower

APP Price Action: At the time of writing, Applovin shares are trading 8.47% lower at $418.00, according to data from Benzinga Pro.
